assumptions: The question concerns hedge accounting for a foreign currency exposure identified in consolidated financial statements.
The relevant source framework is limited to IFRS 9 Section 6 and IFRIC 16 as provided in the context.

approaches.label: Hedge of foreign currency risk on an intragroup monetary item
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 separately addresses foreign currency risk on intragroup monetary items in consolidated financial statements, making it a distinct hedge accounting approach relevant to the issue.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge of foreign currency risk on a forecast intragroup transaction
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 separately addresses cash flow hedges of foreign currency risk on forecast intragroup transactions in consolidated financial statements, which is part of the same intragroup FX hedge framework.

approaches.label: Hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 and IFRIC 16 identify a separate hedge accounting model for foreign currency risk arising from a net investment in a foreign operation at the consolidated level.
